metadata.description: Deep posterior leg compartment containing flexor hallucis longus and tibialis posterior.
Emergent surgical release of leg compartments to restore perfusion.
Delta pressure framework relating diastolic pressure to compartment pressure.
High-risk antecedent injury frequently associated with leg compartment syndrome.
Muscle breakdown with systemic metabolic consequences after ischemia.
Acute elevation of intracompartmental pressure threatening limb perfusion.
Catastrophic outcome after delayed fasciotomy and irreversible ischemia.
Lateral leg compartment containing evertor musculature.
Early clinical sign of compartment ischemia on passive muscle stretch.
Irreversible muscle injury from prolonged compartment ischemia.
Ischemic contracture after untreated compartment syndrome.
Anterior leg compartment containing dorsiflexor musculature.
Four-compartment osteofascial model of the lower leg.
Superficial posterior leg compartment containing gastrocnemius and soleus.

claims.claimText: Leg compartment syndrome requires understanding all four osteofascial compartments — anterior, lateral, deep posterior, and superficial posterior.
Pulselessness is late; pain with passive stretch is earlier and more useful.

metadata.claim_text: Pulselessness is late; pain with passive stretch is earlier and more useful.
metadata.uncertainty_note: Single-source internal draft (deep.boardPearls); requires human verification before learner consumption.

claims.claimText: Never reassure solely from present pulses.

metadata.claim_text: Never reassure solely from present pulses.

claims.claimText: Compartment syndrome is a clinical diagnosis where escalating pain, tense compartments, and pain with passive stretch outweigh waiting for confirmatory imaging.

metadata.claim_text: Compartment syndrome is a clinical diagnosis where escalating pain, tense compartments, and pain with passive stretch outweigh waiting for confirmatory imaging.

claims.claimText: On consult, state time-sensitive limb emergency, repeat compartment exam, loosen dressings, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy.

metadata.claim_text: On consult, state time-sensitive limb emergency, repeat compartment exam, loosen dressings, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy.

claims.claimText: Delaying fasciotomy to obtain imaging or pressure measurements when the clinical picture is evolving.

metadata.claim_text: Delaying fasciotomy to obtain imaging or pressure measurements when the clinical picture is evolving.

claims.claimText: Pain with passive stretch of involved musculature is an earlier and more useful sign than absent pulses.

metadata.claim_text: Pain with passive stretch of involved musculature is an earlier and more useful sign than absent pulses.

claims.claimText: Compartment pressure measurements are adjuncts only — they should not delay treatment when clinical suspicion is high.

metadata.claim_text: Compartment pressure measurements are adjuncts only — they should not delay treatment when clinical suspicion is high.

claims.claimText: Pulselessness is a late finding — do not exclude compartment syndrome when distal pulses are still present.

metadata.claim_text: Pulselessness is a late finding — do not exclude compartment syndrome when distal pulses are still present.

decisionPoints.trigger: Escalating pain out of proportion, tense compartments, pain with passive stretch, or evolving neurovascular deficit
decisionPoints.action: Proceed with emergent leg fasciotomy — do not delay for imaging when clinical suspicion is high

decisionPoints.trigger: Equivocal exam in obtunded patient or unreliable exam conditions
decisionPoints.action: Measure compartment pressures as adjunct; maintain low threshold for fasciotomy if trending abnormal
